word: The smallest unit of language that has a particular meaning and can be expressed by itself; the smallest discrete, meaningful unit of language. (contrast morpheme.)
stroll: A wandering on foot; an idle and leisurely walk; a ramble.
meandering: To wind or turn in a course or passage; to be intricate.
intricate: Having a great deal of fine detail or complexity.
discernible: Possible to discern; detectable or derivable by use of the senses or the intellect.
meandered: To wind or turn in a course or passage; to be intricate.
queen: A female monarch. Example: Queen Victoria.
clock: An instrument used to measure or keep track of time; a non-portable timepiece.
renouncing: To give up, resign, surrender, atsake.
synergy: (systems theory) A synonym of binding energy.
cat: An animal of the family Felidae:
fiddle: Any of various bowed string instruments, often a violin when played in any of various traditional styles, as opposed to classical violin.
lion: A big cat, Panthera leo, native to Africa, India and formerly much of Europe.
dog: A mammal, Canis familiaris or Canis lupus familiaris, that has been domesticated for thousands of years, of highly variable appearance due to human breeding.
melancholy: Black bile, formerly thought to be one of the four "cardinal humours" of animal bodies.
woe: Great sadness or distress; a misfortune causing such sadness.
dream: Imaginary events seen in the mind while sleeping.
goal: A result that one is attempting to achieve.
motto: A sentence, phrase, or word, forming part of an heraldic achievement.
accomplishment: The act of accomplishing; completion; fulfilment.
achievement: The act of achieving or performing; a successful performance; accomplishment
greenhorn: An inexperienced person; a novice, beginner or newcomer
swell: To become bigger, especially due to being engorged.
virtue: Accordance with moral principles; conformity of behaviour or thought with the strictures of morality; good moral conduct.
dignity: The state of being dignified or worthy of esteem: elevation of mind or character.
affiliated: To adopt; to receive into a family as one's offspring
equilibrium: The condition of a system in which competing influences are balanced, resulting in no net change.
admonitions: Gentle or friendly reproof; counseling against fault or oversight; warning.
brittle: A confection of caramelized sugar and nuts.
dystopian: Of or pertaining to a dystopia.
dystopia: A vision of a future that is a corrupted (usually beyond recognition) utopian society.
utopian: Someone who supports or heralds the establishment of a utopia.
utopia: A world in which everything and everyone works in perfect harmony.
eugenics: A social philosophy or practice which advocates the improvement of human hereditary qualities through selective breeding, either by encouraging people with good genetic qualities to reproduce (positive eugenics), or discouraging people with bad genetic qualities from reproducing (negative eugenics), or by technological means.
erode: To wear away by abrasion, corrosion or chemical reaction.
abrasion: The act of abrading, wearing, or rubbing off; the wearing away by friction.
justification: A reason, explanation, or excuse which provides convincing, morally acceptable support for behavior or for a belief or occurrence.
squiggle: A short twisting or wiggling line or mark
pounce: A type of fine powder, as of sandarac, or cuttlefish bone, sprinkled over wet ink to dry the ink after writing or on rough paper to smooth the writing surface.
pounced: To sprinkle or rub with pounce powder.
contented: To give contentment or satisfaction; to satisfy; to make happy.
anticipating: To act before (someone), especially to prevent an action.
allure: The power to attract, entice; the quality causing attraction.
salvation: The process of being saved, the state of having been saved (from hell).
reconcile: To restore a friendly relationship; to bring back to harmony.
vociferous: Making or characterized by a noisy outcry; clamorous.
dogma: An authoritative principle, belief or statement of opinion, especially one considered to be absolutely true and indisputable, regardless of evidence or without evidence to support it.
tentative: A trial; an experiment; an attempt.
corollaries: Something given beyond what is actually due; something added or superfluous.
doctrine: A belief or tenet, especially about philosophical or theological matters.
no: A negating expression; an answer that shows disagreement or disapproval
tea: A drug smoked or ingested for euphoric effect, cannabis.
prevailing: To be superior in strength, dominance, influence or frequency; to have or gain the advantage over others; to have the upper hand; to outnumber others.
wring: A powerful squeezing or twisting action.
nausea: A feeling of illness or discomfort in the digestive system, usually characterized by a strong urge to vomit.
sorcerers: A magician or wizard, sometimes specifically male.
dawdled: To spend time idly and unfruitfully, to waste time.
plunging: To thrust into liquid, or into any penetrable substance; to immerse.
exhilarating: To cheer, to cheer up, to gladden, to make happy.
awestruck: Filled or overcome with awe or wonder.
emissary: An agent sent on a mission to represent the interests of someone else.
prise: The cost required to gain possession of something.
edifice: A building; a structure; an architectural fabric, especially a large and spectacular one
besieged: To beset or surround with armed forces for the purpose of compelling to surrender, to lay siege to, beleaguer.
plagiarised: To use, and pass off as one's own, someone else's writing, speech, ideas, or other intellectual or creative work, especially in an academic context; to commit plagiarism.
ardor: Great warmth of feeling; fervor; passion.
zeal: The fervour or tireless devotion for a person, cause, or ideal and determination in its furtherance; diligent enthusiasm; powerful interest.
charlatans: A mountebank, someone who addresses crowds in the street; (especially), an itinerant seller of medicines or drugs.
avaricious: Actuated by avarice; extremely greedy for wealth or material gain; immoderately desirous of accumulating property.
curtail: A scroll termination, as of a step, etc.
abominable: Worthy of, or causing, abhorrence, as a thing of evil omen; odious in the utmost degree; very hateful; detestable; loathsome; execrable.
voyage: A long journey, especially by ship.
shores: Land adjoining a non-flowing body of water, such as an ocean, lake or pond.
ointment: A viscous preparation of oils and/or fats, usually containing medication, used as a treatment or as an emollient.
stem: The stock of a family; a race or generation of progenitors.
aberration: The act of wandering; deviation from truth, moral rectitude; abnormal; divergence from the straight, correct, proper, normal, or from the natural state.
misdemeanours: A crime usually punishable upon conviction by a small fine or by a short term of imprisonment. In the USA, misdemeanants usually are incarcerated in county jail for less than one year, but felons usually are incarcerated in state or federal prison for more than one year. Crimes which are punishable by large fines or by longer imprisonment are sometimes called felonies.
massacred: To kill in considerable numbers where little or no resistance can be made, with indiscriminate violence, without necessity, and contrary to civilized norms. (Often limited to the killing of human beings.)
rampant: Rearing on both hind legs with the forelegs extended.
colossal: Extremely large or on a great scale.
cornucopia: A goat's horn endlessly overflowing with fruit, flowers and grain; or full of whatever its owner wanted.
turmoil: A state of great disorder or uncertainty.
annihilate: To reduce to nothing, to destroy, to eradicate.
heyday: A period of success, popularity, or power; prime.
tenacious: Clinging to an object or surface; adhesive.
armageddon: The scene of a decisive conflict on a great scale.
decimation: (strictly) The killing or punishment of every tenth person, usually by lot.
